title: What Kind of Strategy Can Generate a $40K Trading Day?
author: Zealousideal-Hat4801
selftext:
My reason for being here is simple: to share my experiences and to assist those who share my mindset.

I do not come from a financial background. I once worked as a supermarket manager, earning just enough to barely scrape by, with virtually no savings to my name. Eventually, I realized that if I wanted to change my circumstances, I had to make different choices  so I quit my job and dedicated myself entirely to market research and trading.

Over time, I gradually developed a structured day-trading methodology that focuses on trends, timing, and discipline, rather than on blind speculation.

The core of this strategy lies in capturing high-probability breakout opportunities within established uptrends  confirming the trend using an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) system, and combining this with "Price Action" analysis to pinpoint entry points.

Key Technical Parameters:

Trend Confirmation: A bullish bias is established when the 9-period, 20-period, and 50-period Exponential Moving Averages (EMAs) exhibit a "Golden Bull" alignment (i.e., shorter-period averages sit above longer-period averages), and the price consistently trades above the rising trendline.

Key Breakout Point (1/4): The focus here is on the price breaking through a previous high or an intraday resistance level, accompanied by a significant surge in trading volume; this indicates that the current market momentum is being sustained.

Optimal Retracement Point (3): The retracement phase following a successful breakout often presents the best opportunity to enter a trade. At this juncture, one should look for a phenomenon known as "Technical Confluence" specifically, when the price pulls back to the intersection point of the rising trendline and a cluster of EMAs (e.g., the 20-period and 50-period EMAs), and finds effective support within that zone.

Target Setting Essentials (5): Set your profit target at a specific price level where the potential upside move is equidistant to the magnitude of the preceding price surge, or alternatively, set it at the next major resistance zone. Trading, for me, has never been about chasing quick wins. It’s about building a repeatable process and executing it consistently over time.

This framework is something I’ve refined through a lot of trial and error, and it’s helped me stay focused on high-probability setups instead of noise.

I’ve organized my full setup and configurations into a simple folder. If you’re working on similar strategies or exploring low-priced stocks and intraday setups, I’m always open to exchanging ideas.

Feel free to reach out or drop a comment   happy to share and learn from others as well.

comments.author: Silver_Village676
comments.body: I've already sent you a message. Please send me the folder. Thank you.

comments.author: IS427
comments.body:
My strategy is a lot like that but what I do is write random letters on a fan blade and use my hand to stop the fan. I string together letters until I get a ticker symbol, and then go all in on that. 

I wouldn’t recommend it I’ve lost 3 fingers.

replies.author: Zealousideal-Hat4801
replies.body: But I prefer setups I can repeat without losing fingers.

replies.author: IS427
replies.body:
Wah wah wah I want all my fingers. 

That’s what you sound like, loser.
